headline: Personal Social Networking via Static Websites
lede: The s@ protocol experiments with shifting the social graph away from centralized servers. By treating your own domain as a data host, you reclaim ownership of your digital presence through encrypted, peer-to-peer verification.

headline: WireGuard Beyond the Tunnel
lede: Moving past its reputation as a VPN, WireGuard functions as a robust encryption layer for UDP. Developers are now decoupling the protocol to secure data streams without the overhead of traditional connection management.

headline: The Nine-Year Quest to Standardize JavaScript Time
lede: For nearly a decade, engineers from across the industry have sought to rectify the architectural flaws inherent in JavaScript’s legacy Date object. The resulting Temporal API introduces immutable, zone-aware logic to replace a system that has frustrated developers since 1995.

headline: Testing the Lifespan of Rewritable Optical Discs
lede: We tend to treat digital storage as an abstract permanence, but physical media follows a precise cycle of decay. Through rigorous automation and computer vision, this study records the exact moment modern DVD±RW discs succumb to the fatigue of repeated writes.

headline: Standardizing the WebAssembly Component Model
lede: WebAssembly is moving beyond its current status as a secondary runtime reliant on JavaScript glue code. By implementing a native component model, the standard aims to provide direct access to browser APIs, finally unlocking true performance parity.

headline: Why Automated Benchmarks Fail Real-World Code Reviews
lede: Passing an automated test is no longer the definitive metric for code quality in the age of LLMs. As researchers found, the gap between functional correctness and professional maintainability remains a hurdle for even the most advanced agentic systems.
pullquote: Functional benchmarks often ignore the structural nuance required for long-term project viability

headline: Managed Device Fleet Compromised in Medical Facility Attack
lede: Hackers have weaponized legitimate administrative tools to initiate a massive wipe of Stryker’s internal corporate devices. The incident underscores the severe risk of relying on single-point-of-failure management software for critical healthcare supply chain operations.

headline: Hacker News Bans AI-Edited Comments
lede: The community has officially codified its stance against the automation of discourse. By prohibiting AI-generated content, administrators are attempting to preserve the site's reputation as a rare bastion of authentic, human-to-human technical exchange.

headline: The Automated Interview Departs from Human Standards
lede: As firms increasingly deploy AI avatars to conduct initial candidate screenings, the personal touch of recruitment is being replaced by scalable, synthetic evaluation. This shift prioritizes operational efficiency over the nuanced, interactive assessment once central to technical hiring.

headline: Data-Oriented Design Achieves Throughput Breakthrough
lede: By shifting from traditional object-oriented patterns to data-centric optimization, engineers successfully resolved locking contention in the Matrix Rust SDK. The resulting refactor provides a clear template for high-performance UI responsiveness in data-heavy applications.
